channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [GKENERGY] GK Energy Ltd Announces Key Appointments and Committee Reconstitution
item.description:
GK Energy Ltd, formerly known as GK Energy Private Limited and GK Energy Marketers Private Limited, held a Board Meeting on March 14, 2026. The meeting resulted in several significant decisions. Firstly, Mr. Shubham Suresh Jain was appointed as Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, and designated as Key Management Personnel. Secondly, Mr. Ramawatar Suresh Laddad was appointed as Assistant General Manager- Accounts and designated as Senior Management Personnel. The Board also authorized Mr. Gopal Rajaram Kabra, Mr. Sunil Kamalkishor Malu, and Mr. Shubham Suresh Jain to determine the materiality of events or information under SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015. Lastly, the Stakeholders Relationship Committee, Corporate Social Responsibility Committee, and Executive Committee were reconstituted.

  • Appointment of Mr. Shubham Suresh Jain as Company Secretary and Compliance Officer
  • Appointment of Mr. Ramawatar Suresh Laddad as Assistant General Manager- Accounts
  • Authorization of officials to determine materiality of events or information
  • Reconstitution of Stakeholders Relationship Committee, Corporate Social Responsibility Committee, and Executive Committee

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: GK Energy Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [INERTIAST] Chief Financial Officer, Mr. Santosh Baburao Ugale, Resigns from Banganga Paper Industries Ltd
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Banganga Paper Industries Ltd has announced the resignation of its Chief Financial Officer, Mr. Santosh Baburao Ugale, effective from 14.03.2026. Mr. Ugale, who was appointed on 22.10.2024, has been leading the company's financial strategy and ensuring compliance with regulatory standards. His contributions have significantly strengthened financial controls and operational efficiency, driving overall growth and stability. Mr. Ugale is not related to any Director of the company and is not debarred from holding the office of Director by virtue of any SEBI order or any other such authority. The company has assured that Mr. Ugale will ensure a smooth transition and will assist in the handover of responsibilities until his departure.

  • Mr. Santosh Baburao Ugale has resigned as Chief Financial Officer of Banganga Paper Industries Ltd.
  • His resignation will be effective from 14.03.2026.
  • Mr. Ugale was appointed as the Chief Financial Officer on 22.10.2024.
  • He has significantly strengthened financial controls and operational efficiency during his tenure.
  • Mr. Ugale has assured a smooth transition and will assist in the handover of responsibilities until his departure.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: INERTIA STEEL LTD. | Category: Management Change

item.title: [SJS] S.J.S. Enterprises Ltd Receives Approval for Scheme of Amalgamation between Subsidiaries
item.description:
S.J.S. Enterprises Ltd has received approval for a Scheme of Amalgamation involving M/s. Plastoranger Advanced Technologies Private Limited and M/s. Walter Pack Automotive Products India Private Limited. The Regional Director, Western Region, Ministry of Corporate Affairs, has approved the Scheme under Section 233 of the Companies Act, 2013. The Scheme has become effective from the date of approval by the Registrar of Companies (RoC). The Appointed Date of the Scheme is April 1, 2025.

  • S.J.S. Enterprises Ltd receives approval for a Scheme of Amalgamation between subsidiaries
  • The Scheme involves M/s. Plastoranger Advanced Technologies Private Limited and M/s. Walter Pack Automotive Products India Private Limited
  • The Scheme has been approved by the Regional Director, Western Region, Ministry of Corporate Affairs
  • The Scheme has become effective from the date of approval by the Registrar of Companies (RoC)
  • The Appointed Date of the Scheme is April 1, 2025

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: S.J.S. Enterprises Ltd | Category: Acquisitions

item.title: [HITECHWIND] Gujarat Winding Systems Ltd Announces Resignation of Director Ms. Ashaben Daujibhai Sharma
item.description:
Gujarat Winding Systems Ltd (Formerly known as Hi-Tech Winding Systems Limited) has announced the resignation of Ms. Ashaben Daujibhai Sharma as Director of the Company with effect from March 14, 2026. The Board of Directors has considered and accepted her resignation. The Company has placed on record its appreciation for the valuable contribution, support and guidance provided by Ms. Ashaben Daujibhai Sharma during her tenure as Directors of the Company.

  • Ms. Ashaben Daujibhai Sharma has resigned from the position of Director of Gujarat Winding Systems Ltd.
  • The Board of Directors has considered and accepted her resignation with effect from March 14, 2026.
  • The Company has appreciated Ms. Ashaben Daujibhai Sharma's contribution during her tenure as Director.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hi-Tech Winding Systems Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [INDIGO] IndiGo Cautiously Aligns Middle East Operations Amid Evolving Situation
item.description:
IndiGo, India's leading airline, is adjusting its operations in the Middle East in response to the evolving situation in the region. The airline will operate 252 weekly flights to/from the Middle East, with 126 weekly flights to/from Saudia Arabia, 28 weekly flights to/from Oman, and 98 weekly flights to/from UAE. The adjustments will be in effect from 16 March 2026 till 28 March 2026. IndiGo is monitoring the situation due to geopolitical risk, airspace restrictions, airport constraints, rising fuel and insurance costs, and other uncertainties. The airline is aligning capacity with the current conditions while maintaining essential connectivity. IndiGo has also suspended flights to Doha, Kuwait, Bahrain, Dammam, Fujairah, Ras Al Khaimah, and Sharjah till 28 March 2026.

  • IndiGo adjusting operations in the Middle East amid evolving situation
  • 252 weekly flights to/from the Middle East
  • 126 weekly flights to/from Saudia Arabia, 28 weekly flights to/from Oman, and 98 weekly flights to/from UAE
  • Suspension of flights to Doha, Kuwait, Bahrain, Dammam, Fujairah, Ras Al Khaimah, and Sharjah till 28 March 2026
  • IndiGo monitoring the situation due to geopolitical risk, airspace restrictions, airport constraints, rising fuel and insurance costs, and other uncertainties

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: InterGlobe Aviation Ltd | Category: Business Update

item.title: [NUVOCO] Nuvoco Vistas Corporation Ltd Receives Assessment Order for FY 2022-23 with Proposed Demand of INR 11.89 Crores
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Nuvoco Vistas Corporation Ltd has received an assessment order from the Faceless Assessing Officer, Assessment Unit, Income Tax, Mumbai for the Financial Year 2022-2023. The order proposes a demand of INR 11.89 crores due to certain disallowances. These disallowances are in line with previous years' orders, and the management is reviewing the order to take appropriate action. The company is confident of getting this order set aside at a higher adjudicating forum, relying on precedents and various court decisions.

  • Nuvoco Vistas Corporation Ltd has received an assessment order for FY 2022-23
  • The order proposes a demand of INR 11.89 crores
  • Disallowances are in line with previous years' orders
  • The management is reviewing the order
  • The company is confident of getting the order set aside at a higher adjudicating forum

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Nuvoco Vistas Corporation Ltd | Category: Legal

item.title: [MITSHI] Mitshi India Ltd Confirms Resignation of Two Directors
item.description:
Mitshi India Ltd has confirmed the resignation of Mr. Hanumant Anaji Salunkhe and Drashti Tarun Savla from their positions as Directors of the company. The resignation was submitted on 10th March, 2026. The company has clarified that there are no other material reasons for their resignation other than those already disclosed in their resignation letters. Neither of the resigned directors hold directorship in any other listed entity or company.

  • Mitshi India Ltd confirms the resignation of Mr. Hanumant Anaji Salunkhe and Drashti Tarun Savla
  • Resignation submitted on 10th March, 2026
  • No other material reasons for resignation apart from those disclosed in resignation letters
  • Neither of the resigned directors hold directorship in any other listed entity or company

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Mitshi India Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [KAYPOWR] Fire Incident at Kay Power and Paper Ltd's Premises; No Casualties Reported
item.description:
A fire incident occurred on 12th March 2026 at Kay Power and Paper Ltd's premises in Satara, India. The fire started in the company's external open storage facility used for wastepaper storage and adjacent machineries. It is believed that the fire originated from the premises of an agricultural school and spread to the company's facility. Although the exact cause is yet to be determined, the fire was controlled by the company's internal team using in-house firefighting systems. There were no human casualties or injuries. The raw material stock and machineries at the affected location are insured, and the company has informed the insurance company. An insurance survey is expected to evaluate the loss.

  • Fire incident at Kay Power and Paper Ltd's premises in Satara, India
  • Fire occurred in the external open storage facility and affected some machineries
  • Fire believed to have originated from an agricultural school's premises
  • No human casualties or injuries reported
  • Raw material stock and machineries adequately insured; insurance survey expected

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: KAY POWER AND PAPER LTD. | Category: Business Update

item.title: [SONATSOFTW] Sonata Software Appoints Balaji Kumar as Chief Human Resources Officer
item.description:
Sonata Software Ltd has appointed Mr. Balaji Kumar as the Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO) with effect from 14 March, 2026. He will be responsible for the People and Talent function globally and will be based out of the Company’s Corporate Office at Bengaluru, India. Balaji rejoins Sonata from One97 Communications (Paytm) where he held the position of Chief Human Resources Officer. He has diverse experience across the People and Talent function including labour & employment laws, talent management, organization development and Business Partnering. He also possesses a master’s degree in law and has practiced law earlier.

  • Appointment of Mr. Balaji Kumar as Chief Human Resources Officer of Sonata Software Ltd
  • Balaji Kumar to be responsible for People and Talent function globally
  • Balaji Kumar's diverse experience in People and Talent function
  • Balaji Kumar's previous experience at One97 Communications (Paytm), L&T Infotech, Larsen & Toubro Ltd, Microsoft, Murugappa Group, Citibank, Coca-Cola and Unilever
  • Balaji Kumar's educational background including a Management Graduate from Tata Institute of Social Sciences and a master’s degree in law

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SONATA SOFTWARE LTD. | Category: Management Change

item.title: [GPIL] Godawari Power and Ispat Ltd Passes Amendment in Object Clause for Logistics Activities
item.description:
Godawari Power and Ispat Ltd held an Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) on 14.03.2026. The meeting was conducted under Regulation 44 (3) of SEBI (LODR) Regulations 2015. The meeting was held to consider the amendment in the Object Clause of the Memorandum of Association of the Company to enable the company to undertake logistics activities. The resolution was passed by a requisite majority with 94.4861% of the votes in favor and 100.0000% against. The meeting was attended by 94.4861% of the total shareholders on record date. The scrutinizer for the meeting was Brajesh R. Agrawal from BR Agrawal & Associates.

  • Godawari Power and Ispat Ltd held an Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) on 14.03.2026
  • The meeting was conducted under Regulation 44 (3) of SEBI (LODR) Regulations 2015
  • The resolution was passed by a requisite majority with 94.4861% of the votes in favor and 100.0000% against
  • The meeting was attended by 94.4861% of the total shareholders on record date
  • The scrutinizer for the meeting was Brajesh R. Agrawal from BR Agrawal & Associates

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: GODAWARI POWER & ISPAT LTD. | Category: Board Meeting
